thinking:
**Analyzing UI Styles**

I've been examining the styling of the OTP input modal. I've noted the dark background and border colors, as well as a shadow using a specific red, and the border/background colors for the close button. Further investigation of the submit button is in progress.


**Matching Website Theme**

I'm now focusing on adapting the OTP input modal to the website's visual style. My analysis indicates a gradient background, rounded cards with white borders and shadows, a slate/blue color palette, and specific input and button stylings. Based on this, I plan to redesign the modal's background, card appearance, text colors, and button appearance to align with the website's design elements and branding.


**Refining Modal Appearance**

I'm now revising the OTP input modal's appearance for a modern look. Instead of a dark background, I'm using a frosted glass aesthetic with `flex items-center justify-center`. The modal container now has a white background, rounded corners, a subtle shadow, and a blurred backdrop, all based on the existing site. I'm swapping the close button for a Lucide `X` icon, and updating the heading and subtitle styles. I'm exploring input styling to match!


**Updating Input Fields**

I've discarded the clashing red neon shadow and implemented Tailwind classes for input styling. I'm focusing on `w-12 h-12`, with `text-center text-xl font-bold text-slate-800 bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-2xl outline-none transition-all duration-200 focus:border-blue-500 focus:ring-4 focus:ring-blue-500/10 shadow-sm`. I'm using `w-12 h-12` (48px) for input size. Also, I'm updating error text to `text-rose-500 font-medium mt-1 mb-4`. The resend code will be a styled button, and the timer text with appropriate styling.


**Confirming Button Style**

I'm now confirming the submit button style matches the signup page's "Create Account" button. It's a perfect alignment! The button uses `mt-6 w-full py-3 bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-700 text-white font-semibold rounded-xl transition cursor-pointer shadow-lg shadow-blue-500/10`. I'm verifying the necessary Lucide import, and will make sure the `X` is imported. I have confirmed that importing `X` will be added to the project, and then I am reviewing the whole file now.


**Revising Button Interactions**

Now, I'm focusing on refining the interactive elements within the modal. I've styled the resend button with red text and a cursor pointer for easy access. The timer now has a gray color that changes to red when a user did not receive the code. The submit button is now styled with a rounded border and a hover effect for a better user experience.


**Verifying Imports and Styles**

I've just confirmed the implementation details of the modal components. The visual styling is exactly what I was hoping to see! Specifically, the frosted glass effect, the card background, text and input colors, and buttons match perfectly. My analysis also confirms the correct use of the `X` icon from Lucide, ensuring it's positioned correctly within the layout. There's no import statement for the `X` icon, and I need to add it!

args.Description: "Change OtpInputModal styling to match the clean, white glassmorphism theme of the website with blue/indigo highlights instead of dark red/black."
args.Instruction: "Update the entire OtpInputModal implementation with light glassmorphism styles, clean blue inputs, X close icon, and a blue premium primary submit button."
args.toolSummary: "Replace OtpInputModal with modern light glass theme"
